methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $260M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Last Energy, a Series C nuclear energy company founded by Bret Kugelmass, has raised $260M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy firms—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private players including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression methodology applies here, using the $260M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $442M. Peer-median comparison is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow nuclear energy set; reliance on broader-sector medians would introduce unwarranted noise.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $376M (low) to $508M (high), with a median point of $442M. This range reflects inherent uncertainties around: (1) the precision of stage-progression multiples in a sector with limited transaction data, (2) heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity across the peer set, and (3) the absence of robust market multiples in early-stage advanced nuclear. The analysis is anchored to a single method; broader validation would require deeper private-market transaction history or tighter peer clustering by sub-technology or deployment timeline.
